  "findings": [
    "The tension in each sling leg (approx. 4,359 lb) exceeds the tagged vertical Working Load Limit (3,500 lb) of the sling."
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Do not proceed with the lift using this rigging configuration.",
    "Select slings with a higher Working Load Limit (WLL) or increase the horizontal sling angle to reduce the tension per leg."
  ],
  "rationale": "For a symmetric two-leg sling arrangement, the tension in each leg is calculated as: Tension = (Load / 2) / sin(angle). Here, Tension = (5,000 lb / 2) / sin(35 degrees) = 2,500 / 0.57358 = 4,358.6 lb. Since 4,358.6 lb exceeds the rated capacity of 3,500 lb, the configuration is unsafe and non-compliant.",
  "references": [
    "ASME B30.9",
    "OSHA 1926.251"
  ]
}
